title: Richly Flavored Korean Monkfish Bake
description: A light and citrusy Korean dish featuring monkfish, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this satvik-friendly recipe is a delight to the taste buds.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the monkfish fillets and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a blender or food processor, combine lemon juice, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, and brown sugar, and blend until smooth.
Place the monkfish fillets in a baking dish and brush the citrus sauce evenly over both sides of the fish.
Sprinkle the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds over the top of the fish.
Bake the monkfish in the preheated oven for 12 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the monkfish from the oven and let it rest for 2 minutes before serving.
Serve the monkfish hot, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
